title: andropogon fasciculatus
intro: Andropogon fasciculatus, also known as East Indian stevia or sugar bush, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Poaceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, as well as its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of A. fasciculatus include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce blood sugar levels, and act as a natural sweetener due to its high steviol glycoside content. A. fasciculatus is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
